purpose: Technical manifest for a future INPI software registration filing. This manifest is not the filing itself and does not submit data to INPI.

official_sources_checked.name: INPI Guia básico de programa de computador
INPI Programa de computador - Mais informações

inpi_requirements_summary.technical_documentation_guardian: Rights holder/applicant keeps the confidential technical documentation and must preserve its integrity.

software.name: CaseHub
software.category: Legal operations SaaS / legal case management platform
software.snapshot_focus: Conservative identification snapshot for software-registration evidence, not a runnable deployment package. It contains allowlisted CaseHub Basic application/source paths only; repo-wide legacy services, operational scripts, docs, generated evidence, reports, archived template notes, template backup files, generated static reports, and customer contact data are outside this snapshot.
main_modules: Authentication, roles, sessions, and two-factor controls
Client, case, process, task, calendar, deadline, and file management
CRM, leads, billing, invoice, and relationship workflows
Document templates, legal tools, packets, signatures, and generated pieces
Notifications, communications, email, and Google Calendar integration surfaces
Admin, audit, onboarding, settings, branding, reporting, and workflow routes
Multi-tenant OAuth (Google SSO, Drive, Calendar, Gmail per-org)
Integrations gateway (synthetic adapters, credential store, admin status route)
WhatsApp multi-session bot integration via proxy + per-org bot instances
Onboarding subdomain + slug validation + reserved blocklist (Fatia A)
Performance, deployment, security, and packaging support files for the application

snapshot_artifact.generated_with: Python 3.x zipfile, sorted paths, fixed timestamps (1980-01-01), ZIP_STORED compression for byte-stable reproducibility
snapshot_artifact.sha256_scope: Preserved ZIP artifact at local_path_at_preparation. If the archive is regenerated later, recalculate the hash and update this manifest before filing.
snapshot_artifact.not_committed_reason: The source snapshot is confidential technical documentation for filing evidence; the repository preserves the manifest only.

notable_additions_since_previous: PR #637 merge: OAuth multi-tenant refactor (per-org credentials, JWT subdomain scoping)
PR #637 sibling fixes: T1/T2/T6/T11 security mitigations (JWT scope, email org_id, account_name sanitization, /uploads auth)
PR #650 merge: integrations-gateway admin route + credential store + synthetic adapters
PR #651 merge: onboarding Fatia A — subdomain schema + reserved blocklist + slug validation API
PR #648 merge: CI restore — deploy-alpha smoke 90s + rollback + onboarding migration step
PR #649 merge: audit-prod-review target rename prod=dev
PR #653 merge: T3 IDOR hotfix in email_templates_v2 (cases lookup org_id filter)

red_lines: Do not submit the filing from automation.
Do not send the snapshot over open WhatsApp or public chat.
Do not store raw credentials, production .env files, client documents, or generated private uploads in the registration artifact.
Do not expand the allowlist to legacy services, operational scripts, or customer-contact material without human/legal review.
Confirm trademark step A5 and applicant/titular data before protocol.
next_human_steps: Move the allowlisted source ZIP artifact from /tmp to private redundant storage controlled by the rights holder.
Confirm applicant and rights holder data for the designated legal entity (LegalOps Co. LTDA).
Generate and sign the INPI Declaração de Veracidade with the correct ICP-Brasil certificate.
Copy the SHA-256 hash and algorithm into the e-Software form when the designated representative is ready to file.
Wait until A5 (trademark registration step) is complete before protocoling — this is the documented dependency from issue #413.
